statement.p: An old cookbook has a recipe for soup that uses 15 g of flour for thickener. The recipe makes 1.75 gal of soup, but you are adjusting it to make only 1.5 gal. How much flour should you use?

solution.p: Let x be how many grams of flour we should use. The amount of flour is proportional to the amount of soup, so we can compare the ratios of flour to soup for our batch with the original recipe:
These denominators are not whole numbers, and we decide not to try using the least common multiple. Instead, we just multiply on each side by both denominators:
So we should use {12.8571\ {\rm g}} of flour.
